    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT plan_handle) > (SELECT COUNT(*) * 2 FROM sys.databases)
 ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT plan_handle) DESC
     )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Many Plans for One Query' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Many Plans for One Query' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: sys.dm_exec_cached_plans , counts query_hash values with many distinct plan_handle entries. Multiple plans for the same query indicate parameter sensitivity or implicit type conversion preventing plan reuse. Use sys.dm_exec_query_plan to compare plans and identify the differing elements. Candidates for OPTION(OPTIMIZE FOR) or Query Store plan forcing.
FullStoryAction: This might point to performance problems

Name: High Number of Cached Plans
Description: The procedure cache contains an unusually high number of cached plans. A bloated plan cache consumes significant memory that could otherwise be used for data pages, and can slow plan lookups during query compilation.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT TOP 1 161 AS CheckID,
 100 AS Priority,
 'Performance' AS FindingsGroup,
 'High Number of Cached Plans' AS Finding,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/planlimits' AS URL,
 'Your server configuration is limited to ' + CAST(ht.buckets_count * 4 AS VARCHAR(20)) + ' ' + ht.name + ', and you are currently caching ' + CAST(cc.entries_count AS VARCHAR(20)) + '.' AS Details
 FROM sys.dm_os_memory_cache_hash_tables ht
 INNER JOIN sys.dm_os_memory_cache_counters cc ON ht.name = cc.name AND ht.type = cc.type
 where ht.name IN ( 'SQL Plans' , 'Object Plans' , 'Bound Trees' )
 AND cc.entries_count >= (3 * ht.buckets_count)
     )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'High Number of Cached Plans' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'High Number of Cached Plans' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: sys.dm_exec_cached_plans WHERE usecounts > 1 , high plan count indicates cache pressure or excessive parameterisation variety. Check plan cache via: SELECT TOP 10 usecounts, size_in_bytes, cacheobjtype, objtype, sys.dm_exec_sql_text(plan_handle) FROM sys.dm_exec_cached_plans CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(plan_handle) ORDER BY usecounts DESC.
FullStoryAction: SQL server has a limit to the number of plans that can be stored in the Plan Cache.

Name: Query Store Disabled
Description: Query Store is disabled on one or more user databases. Query Store tracks query performance history, execution plans, and runtime statistics, enabling identification of plan regressions. It should be enabled on all production databases.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_query_store_options WHERE desired_state = 0 AND actual_state = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Query Store Disabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Query Store Disabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: sys.databases where is_query_store_on = 0. Query Store is not enabled. Query Store (SQL 2016+) captures query plans and runtime statistics automatically. Without it: no plan regression detection, no plan forcing, no historical performance baseline. Enable: ALTER DATABASE [db] SET QUERY_STORE = ON (READ_WRITE) WITH (MAX_STORAGE_SIZE_MB = 2048, QUERY_CAPTURE_MODE = AUTO).
FullStoryAction: The SQL Server 2016+ Query Store feature can impact performance

Name: Plan Guides Failing
Description: One or more plan guides are failing to apply. When a plan guide fails, SQL Server silently falls back to the optimizer's default plan selection. Failing plan guides indicate that the query signature has changed and the guide no longer matches.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.plan_guides WHERE is_disabled = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Plan Guides Failing' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Plan Guides Failing' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckClearSimplified: Plan guides, if any, seem to be working
NextAction: sys.plan_guides WHERE is_disabled = 0 , a plan guide exists but is failing (plan guide match fails and query runs without the guide). When a plan guide fails silently, the intended query plan is not applied. Check: SELECT * FROM sys.plan_guides WHERE is_disabled = 0. Test: use SET SHOWPLAN_ALL ON and verify the guide is being used. Repair or drop broken plan guides.
FullStoryAction: There are cases when database plan guides are no longer valid, this can cause queries to be failing.

Name: Too Much Free Memory
Description: SQL Server has an unusually large amount of free (uncommitted) memory relative to the server's configured maximum. This may indicate that the max server memory setting is too high relative to what workloads actually need, or that workloads have recently decreased.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT 165, 50, 'Performance', 'Too Much Free Memory', 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/freememory',
  CAST((CAST(cFree.cntr_value AS BIGINT) / 1024 / 1024 ) AS NVARCHAR(100)) + N'GB of free memory inside SQL Server''s buffer pool, which is ' + CAST((CAST(cTotal.cntr_value AS BIGINT) / 1024 / 1024) AS NVARCHAR(100)) + N'GB. You would think lots of free memory would be good, but check out the URL for more information.' AS Details
 FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters cFree
 INNER JOIN sys.dm_os_performance_counters cTotal ON cTotal.object_name LIKE N'%Memory Manager%'
  AND cTotal.counter_name = N'Total Server Memory (KB)                                                                                                        '
 WHERE cFree.object_name LIKE N'%Memory Manager%'
  AND cFree.counter_name = N'Free Memory (KB)                                                                                                                '
  AND CAST(cTotal.cntr_value AS BIGINT) > 20480000000
  AND CAST(cTotal.cntr_value AS BIGINT) * .3 <= CAST(cFree.cntr_value AS BIGINT)
        AND CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('edition') AS VARCHAR(100)) NOT LIKE '%Standard%'
     )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Too Much Free Memory' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Too Much Free Memory' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: @ server(s) have Too Much Free Memory
NextAction: sys.dm_os_process_memory , process working set is significantly lower than committed memory, indicating memory pressure is forcing pages to disk (paging). SQL Server buffer pool is being paged out. Add RAM or reduce max server memory to leave more headroom for the OS.
FullStoryAction: There are instances when SQL server has too much free memory in the SQL memory allocation.  This might not be an ideal situation.

Name: Agent is Currently Offline
Description: SQL Server Agent is currently stopped or offline. Agent being offline means no scheduled jobs will run, including backups, maintenance plans, monitoring jobs, and any ETL or automated processes depending on the Agent.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs WHERE enabled = 1 AND name LIKE '%Agent%' AND name NOT LIKE '%SQL Server Agent%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Agent is Currently Offline' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Agent is Currently Offline' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: SQL Server Agent service status via sys.dm_server_services WHERE servicename LIKE '%Agent%' and status_desc != 'Running'. The SQL Agent is offline. All scheduled jobs (backups, maintenance, alerting) are not running. High-priority fix , restart the Agent service and investigate why it stopped.
FullStoryAction: The SQL Agent allows scheduled maintenance and jobs to be performed. This service should always be running.

Name: Full-Text Filter Daemon Launcher Offline
Description: Full-Text Filter Daemon Launcher is offline.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.dm_server_services
    WHERE [status_desc] <> 'Running'
      AND [servicename] LIKE 'SQL Full-text Filter Daemon Launcher%'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Start the SQL Full-text Filter Daemon Launcher service and set its startup type to Automatic. Check the Windows event log for service startup failures that may indicate underlying issues.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when the Full-text Filter Daemon Launcher is running. Returns FAIL when it is stopped.
BusinessTranslation: Monitoring gaps mean you learn about production issues from users or executives. The mean time to detection goes from seconds to hours.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: Full-text might not be working as expected
CheckClearSimplified: Full-text seems to be working as expected
NextAction: sys.dm_fts_active_catalogs or sys.dm_server_services WHERE servicename LIKE '%Full-text%'. The Full-Text Filter Daemon Launcher service (fdlaunch.exe) is stopped or offline. Without it, full-text index population pauses and full-text queries against indexes that need updating return stale results. Start the service: net start MSSQLFDLauncher (or MSSQLFDLauncher$INSTANCENAME for named instances). Configure it to start automatically.
FullStoryAction: Full-Text Search in SQL Server is used to run full-text queries against data in SQL Server tables.

Name: SQL Server Running as NT Service Account
Description: SQL Server running as NT Service account.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.dm_server_services
    WHERE [service_account] LIKE 'NT Service%'
      AND [servicename] LIKE 'SQL Server%'
      AND [servicename] NOT LIKE 'SQL Server Agent%'
      AND [servicename] NOT LIKE 'SQL Server Launchpad%'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: No action required; this is the expected and secure service account configuration. If a domain account is required for specific needs, make sure it follows the principle of least privilege.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when SQL Server uses an NT Service account (informational). Returns FAIL otherwise.
BusinessTranslation: Monitoring gaps mean you learn about production issues from users or executives. The mean time to detection goes from seconds to hours.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: SQL Server is running under an NT Service account
CheckClearSimplified: SQL Server is running under an domain Service account
NextAction: sys.columns where encryption_type IS NOT NULL. Column-level encryption (CLE via EncryptByKey/EncryptByCert, or Always Encrypted via column master key). Informational , confirms encryption is in place for sensitive data. Verify key rotation is scheduled and backup of encryption keys/certificates is current (separate from database backup).
FullStoryAction: SQL Server services can run as NT Service accounts. It is ideal to run as Active Directory accounts.

Name: SQL Agent Running as NT Service Account
Description: SQL Agent running as NT Service account.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.dm_server_services
    WHERE [service_account] LIKE 'NT Service%'
      AND [servicename] LIKE 'SQL Server Agent%'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: No action required; virtual accounts are the secure default for SQL Agent. If a domain account is used for cross-server job steps, make sure it has only necessary permissions.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when SQL Agent uses an NT Service account. Returns FAIL otherwise.
BusinessTranslation: Monitoring gaps mean you learn about production issues from users or executives. The mean time to detection goes from seconds to hours.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: SQL Server Agent is running under an NT Service account
CheckClearSimplified: SQL Server Agent is running under an domain Service account
NextAction: sys.dm_server_services WHERE servicename LIKE '%Agent%' and service_account LIKE 'NT Service%'. SQL Agent is running as the built-in NT Service account. This account has limited network access , it cannot write to network shares, access Active Directory, or perform remote operations required by some jobs (log shipping, SSIS, linked server jobs). Configure a dedicated domain service account with minimum required permissions.
FullStoryAction: SQL Server Agent services can run under NT Service accounts. It is ideal to run as Active Directory accounts.

Name: Non-Production License
Description: Detects when SQL Server is running under a Developer or Evaluation edition license. Developer Edition is free for development and testing but is not licensed for production use. Evaluation Edition has a 180-day time limit after which SQL Server will stop working. Running either in production violates Microsoft licensing terms and puts your application at risk of unexpected shutdown.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    WHERE CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('edition') AS VARCHAR(100)) LIKE '%Developer%'
       OR CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('edition') AS VARCHAR(100)) LIKE '%Evaluation%'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Upgrade to a licensed edition (Standard or Enterprise) before deploying to production.
If this is a development or test server, no action is required.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when a production-licensed edition is in use. Returns FAIL when Developer or Evaluation edition is detected.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: Some SQL servers are incorrectly licensed. There is a risk of financial liability. Take urgent action.
NextAction: SERVERPROPERTY('Edition') contains 'Developer', 'Express', or 'Evaluation'. A non-production licence running a production workload violates Microsoft licensing terms and may have capacity limits (Express: 10 GB database size cap, 1 socket / 4 cores). Evaluate whether the correct production licence is in place.
FullStoryAction: Running production workloads will incur Licensing fees

Name: Buffer Pool Extensions Enabled
Description: Buffer Pool Extension (BPE) is enabled. BPE uses a fast SSD as a second-tier cache for the buffer pool, extending its effective size. This feature can help on systems with insufficient RAM but adds complexity and may not provide expected benefits.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_os_buffer_pool_extension_configuration) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Buffer Pool Extensions Enabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Buffer Pool Extensions Enabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: Buffer Pool Extensions Enabled on @ server(s)
NextAction: sp_configure 'buffer pool extension enabled' = 1 (SQL 2014â€“2019 only). Buffer Pool Extension (BPE) uses an SSD as an extension of the buffer pool for caching read-only pages. Deprecated and removed in SQL 2022. Performance benefit is marginal , adding more RAM is almost always better. Monitor via sys.dm_os_buffer_pool_extension_configuration.
FullStoryAction: Buffer Pool Extensions is one of the features that are available, but should ideally not be used.

Name: TempDB Has More Than 16 Data Files
Description: Detects when tempdb has more than 16 data files. SQL Server recommends one tempdb data file per logical processor core, up to a maximum of 8, unless you observe allocation page latch contention (PAGELATCH waits on tempdb files). Having too many tempdb files can actually increase management overhead without improving performance and may indicate a misconfiguration or a runaway script that created excessive files.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.master_files AS mf
    WHERE mf.database_id = 2
      AND mf.[type] = 0
    HAVING COUNT_BIG(*) > 16
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Reduce tempdb data file count to match the number of logical CPU cores, up to a maximum of 8.
Ensure all tempdb data files have identical sizing and autogrowth settings.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when tempdb has 16 or fewer data files. Returns FAIL when more than 16 exist.
NextAction: sys.master_files WHERE database_id = 2 (tempdb) and file_type = 0 (data files) , count exceeds 16. Having more than 8â€“16 tempdb data files provides no additional benefit and complicates management. SQL's proportional fill algorithm becomes less effective with very large file counts. The recommended maximum is 8 files (or equal to logical CPU count if fewer than 8). Extra files beyond the recommended count do not reduce contention and may increase management overhead.
FullStoryAction: There are recommendations from Microsoft around TempDB file configuration, these generally taper off at 8 files.

Name: Extended Events Hyperextension
Description: An unusually high number of Extended Events sessions are active. Too many active Extended Events sessions can add overhead to SQL Server operations, as each session must evaluate and capture events matching its configured filters.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_xe_sessions WHERE name NOT LIKE '%system_health%' AND name NOT LIKE '%sp_blocker%' AND name NOT LIKE '%telemetry%' AND create_time > DATEADD(dd, -1, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Extended Events Hyperextension' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Extended Events Hyperextension' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: sys.dm_xe_sessions where name LIKE '%' and event count exceeds a threshold. Extended Events sessions are writing too many events , likely capturing high-frequency events without adequate filtering (e.g. sql_statement_completed without WHERE filter). Review session definitions: SELECT xes.name, xe.event_name FROM sys.dm_xe_sessions xes JOIN sys.dm_xe_session_events xe ON xes.address = xe.event_session_address.
FullStoryAction: Too many Extended Events , just like profile traces, can cause performance problems on SQL servers.

Name: Disabled Internal Monitoring Features
Description: Internal monitoring features disabled.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.dm_server_registry AS dsr
    WHERE dsr.registry_key LIKE N'%MSSQLServer\Parameters'
      AND dsr.value_data = '-x'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Remove the -x startup parameter immediately from SQL Server Configuration Manager. Restart the SQL Server service after removing the parameter to restore internal monitoring.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when -x startup parameter is not present. Returns FAIL when -x is detected.
BusinessTranslation: Monitoring gaps mean you learn about production issues from users or executives. The mean time to detection goes from seconds to hours.
NextAction: sys.configurations WHERE name = 'default trace enabled' = 0 or related internal monitoring feature disabled. Default trace, DMV retention, or other built-in diagnostic features have been disabled. These provide zero-cost forensic data that is invaluable for incident investigation. Re-enable unless replaced with equivalent coverage.
FullStoryAction: Internal Monitoring can be disabled using certain server startup switches. This is not advisable in production environments.

Name: Snapshot Backups Occurring
Description: SQL Server is performing volume snapshot backups (using VSS or third-party snapshot technology). While valid, snapshot backups require proper configuration and may affect SQL Server's log truncation behavior if not properly acknowledged.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 178 AS CheckID ,
 200 AS Priority ,
 'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Snapshot Backups Occurring' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/snaps' AS URL ,
 ( CAST(COUNT(*) AS VARCHAR(20)) + ' snapshot-looking backups have occurred in the last two weeks, indicating that IO may be freezing up.') AS Details
 FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs
 WHERE bs.type = 'D'
 AND bs.backup_size >= 50000000000 /* At least 50GB */
 AND DATEDIFF(SECOND, bs.backup_start_date, bs.backup_finish_date) <= 60 /* Backup took less than 60 seconds */
 AND bs.backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(DAY, -14, GETDATE()) /* In the last 2 weeks */
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Snapshot Backups Occurring' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Snapshot Backups Occurring' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: sys.databases where source_database_id IS NOT NULL (database snapshot). Database snapshots hold a sparse file copy of the original database at the snapshot creation point. They grow as pages change in the source database, consuming disk space. Old snapshots cause source database disk writes to double (CoW mechanism). List and drop stale snapshots: SELECT * FROM sys.databases WHERE source_database_id IS NOT NULL.
FullStoryAction: These backups may cause IO to freeze. Frozen IO effectively pauses all server processing for that moment.

Name: Dangerous Third Party Modules
Description: Third-party modules (DLLs) are loaded into the SQL Server process. Problematic third-party modules such as antivirus, monitoring agents, or backup software loaded into the SQL Server process have been known to cause stability and performance issues.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT name FROM sys.dm_os_loaded_modules WHERE name NOT LIKE '%Microsoft%' AND name NOT LIKE '%SQL%' AND name NOT LIKE '%.dll' AND name NOT LIKE '%sqlservr%' AND company NOT LIKE '%Microsoft%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Dangerous Third Party Modules' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Dangerous Third Party Modules' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: sys.dm_os_loaded_modules WHERE name NOT LIKE '%Microsoft%' and NOT LIKE '%Windows%'. Third-party DLLs loaded in the SQL Server address space (antivirus, backup agents, monitoring tools) can cause access violations, memory corruption, or scheduler non-yielding conditions. Review each module. Configure antivirus to exclude SQL Server process memory scanning.
FullStoryAction: Suspected dangerous third party module may be present, and more details can be found there https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/kb/2033238

Name: Shrink Database Step In Maintenance Plan
Description: A SQL Server Maintenance Plan contains a Shrink Database step. Shrinking databases causes severe index fragmentation requiring immediate rebuilds, and the freed space is often re-consumed quickly, wasting the I/O spent on the shrink operation.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysssispackages WHERE CAST(CAST(packagedata AS VARBINARY(MAX)) AS VARCHAR(MAX)) LIKE '%Shrink Database Task%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Shrink Database Step In Maintenance Plan' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Shrink Database Step In Maintenance Plan' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: Shrink Database Step In Maintenance Plan
NextAction: sys.dm_exec_query_plan , detects maintenance plan steps with DBCC SHRINKDATABASE or SHRINKFILE. A shrink step in a maintenance plan re-fragments every index immediately after the rebuild step. Remove shrink steps from all maintenance plans. If free space is a concern, reclaim it by archiving data or adding storage.
FullStoryAction: When it comes to Maintenance Plans the Database shrink jobs can cause performance degradation on Production systems

Name: Repetitive Steps In Maintenance Plans
Description: SQL Server Maintenance Plans contain redundant steps such as rebuilding and reorganizing indexes on the same database, or running CHECKDB multiple times. These redundant operations waste maintenance window time and system resources.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysssispackages WHERE CAST(CAST(packagedata AS VARBINARY(MAX)) AS VARCHAR(MAX)) LIKE '%Duplicate Steps%' OR CAST(CAST(packagedata AS VARBINARY(MAX)) AS VARCHAR(MAX)) LIKE '%Repetitive Steps%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Repetitive Steps In Maintenance Plans' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Repetitive Steps In Maintenance Plans' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckClearSimplified: No Repetitive Steps In Maintenance Plans
NextAction: msdb.dbo.sysmaintplan_subplans with repetitive steps. SQL Agent maintenance plan XML contains duplicate or overlapping steps (e.g. multiple REBUILD INDEX steps). Maintenance plans are inflexible compared to Ola Hallengren's maintenance solution. Consolidate to a single well-sequenced plan or migrate to IndexOptimize/DatabaseBackup scripts.
FullStoryAction: Standard Maintenance is doing repetitive work on indexes and statistics.

Name: Query Store Cleanup Disabled
Description: Query Store automatic data cleanup is disabled on one or more databases. Without automatic cleanup, Query Store data grows indefinitely and may fill the allocated storage, causing Query Store to switch to read-only mode and stop capturing new data.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_query_store_options WHERE actual_state = 2 AND size_based_cleanup_mode = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Query Store Cleanup Disabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Query Store Cleanup Disabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: Query Store Cleanup Disabled On @ server(s)
NextAction: sys.databases where is_query_store_on = 1 and query_store_state = 'READ_ONLY' because max_storage_size is reached. Query Store stops capturing new data when the size limit is reached. Check: SELECT * FROM sys.database_query_store_options. Fix: increase MAX_STORAGE_SIZE_MB or set CLEANUP_POLICY (STALE_QUERY_THRESHOLD_DAYS). Run: ALTER DATABASE [db] SET QUERY_STORE CLEAR to flush old data if necessary.
FullStoryAction: SQL 2016 RTM has a bug involving dumps that happen every time Query Store cleanup jobs run. This is fixed in CU1 and later.

Name: TempDB Unevenly Sized Data Files
Description: TempDB data files are not all the same size. For proportional fill to distribute I/O evenly across TempDB files, all data files should be equal in size. Unevenly sized files cause SQL Server to disproportionately fill larger files first.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

 SELECT    183 ,
     'tempdb' ,
     170 ,
     'File Configuration' ,
     'TempDB Unevenly Sized Data Files' ,
     'https://www.brentozar.com/go/tempdb' ,
     'TempDB data files are not configured with the same size.  Unevenly sized tempdb data files will result in unevenly sized workloads.'
WHERE ( SELECT COUNT (distinct [size])
FROM   tempdb.sys.database_files
WHERE  type_desc = 'ROWS'
HAVING MAX((size * 8) / (1024. * 1024)) - MIN((size * 8) / (1024. * 1024)) > 1.
       ) <> 1
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'TempDB Unevenly Sized Data Files' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'TempDB Unevenly Sized Data Files' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckClearSimplified: TempDB has evenly Sized Data Files
NextAction: sys.master_files for tempdb , data files are not equal in size. SQL uses proportional fill to distribute writes across tempdb files equally. If files are unequal, the larger file gets proportionally more I/O. Pre-size all tempdb data files equally at startup. After adding files or resizing, restart SQL to reinitialize.
FullStoryAction: Unevenly sized tempdb data files can lead to I/O hotspots or increased disk latency.

Name: No Failover Cluster Nodes Available
Description: A Windows Server Failover Cluster (WSFC) has no available nodes to fail over to. If all cluster nodes are occupied by active SQL Server instances, a failure of the current node cannot be recovered automatically via failover.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN SERVERPROPERTY('IsClustered') = 1 AND NOT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_os_cluster_nodes WHERE is_current_owner = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'No Failover Cluster Nodes Available' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'No Failover Cluster Nodes Available' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: sys.availability_groups where there are no remaining failover-ready replicas (all secondaries are either DISCONNECTED or SYNCHRONIZING). If the primary fails now, manual failover is required (no automatic failover available). Investigate secondary replica connectivity: SELECT replica_server_name, operational_state_desc, connected_state_desc FROM sys.dm_hadr_availability_replica_states.
FullStoryAction: For failover clustering a passive node needs to be available if the event where the active node fails

Name: MSDB Backup History Purged Too Frequently
Description: MSDB backup history is being purged very frequently (possibly daily or more). Overly aggressive history purging removes the ability to verify backup chains, audit backup history, or identify gaps in backup coverage over time.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT TOP 1
 186 AS CheckID ,
 'msdb' ,
 200 AS Priority ,
 'Backup' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'MSDB Backup History Purged Too Frequently' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/history' AS URL ,
 ( 'Database backup history only retained back to '
 + CAST(bs.backup_start_date AS VARCHAR(20)) ) AS Details
FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs
ORDER BY backup_start_date ASC
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'MSDB Backup History Purged Too Frequently' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'MSDB Backup History Purged Too Frequently' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: MSDB Backup History Purged Too Frequently
CheckClearSimplified: Backup history logs are not being purged too frequently
NextAction: msdb.dbo.backupset WHERE backup_finish_date > DATEADD(DAY, -7, GETDATE()) , backup history for a database was purged within the past week. Excessive backup history purges destroy the audit trail needed to determine the last known-good restore point. Set a longer retention policy for msdb backup history: EXEC msdb.dbo.sp_delete_backuphistory with a date further in the past.
FullStoryAction: MSDB Backup History Purged Too Frequently. This means backup history might not be available during a restore operation.

Name: Endpoints Owned by Users
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Endpoints Owned by Users' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS ( 
SELECT
     187 AS [CheckID] ,
     230 AS [Priority] ,
     'Security' AS [FindingsGroup] ,
     'Endpoints Owned by Users' AS [Finding] ,
     'https://www.brentozar.com/go/owners' AS [URL] ,
     ( 'Endpoint ' + ep.[name] + ' is owned by ' + SUSER_NAME(ep.principal_id) + '. If the endpoint owner login is disabled or not available due to Active Directory problems, the high availability will stop working.'
     ) AS [Details]
FROM sys.database_mirroring_endpoints ep
LEFT OUTER JOIN sys.dm_server_services s ON SUSER_NAME(ep.principal_id) = s.service_account
WHERE s.service_account IS NULL AND ep.principal_id <> 1
) AND SERVERPROPERTY('IsHadrEnabled') = 1
THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Endpoints Owned by Users' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Endpoints Owned by Users' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: Data Sync Endpoints are Owned by a User
CheckClearSimplified: Data Sync Endpoints are Owned by the system
NextAction: sys.endpoints where principal_id != sa and the endpoint owner is an unprivileged login. Endpoint ownership allows the owner to change endpoint configuration, potentially enabling unauthenticated connections. Reassign endpoint ownership to sa: ALTER AUTHORIZATION ON ENDPOINT::[name] TO sa.
FullStoryAction: If the endpoint owner login is disabled or not available due to Active Directory problems, the high availability will stop working.

Name: Cost threshold for parallelism
Description: The Cost Threshold for Parallelism is set to an unusually low value (default is 5). A low cost threshold causes many small queries to run in parallel unnecessarily, increasing CXPACKET waits and reducing throughput for concurrent workloads.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'cost threshold for parallelism' AND value_in_use <= 5) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Cost threshold for parallelism' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Cost threshold for parallelism' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: sp_configure 'cost threshold for parallelism' = 5 (default low value). A CTP of 5 means virtually any non-trivial query is eligible for parallelism. On OLTP servers, this causes excessive CXPACKET waits and serial bottlenecks at the thread synchronisation point. Recommended: raise CTP to 25â€“50 for mixed OLTP/OLAP; higher (50â€“100) for pure OLTP. Test changes during off-peak with Query Store plan regression monitoring.
FullStoryAction: Parallelism threshold is set by default to 5. Changing this may reduce CXPACKET waits.

Name: Missing Features
Description: SQL Server is missing recommended features or configurations for its version. This may indicate that version-specific features that improve performance, security, or reliability have not been enabled or configured after installation.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_os_windows_info WHERE windows_release <> '10' AND windows_sku < 7) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Missing Features' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Missing Features' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: TempDB File Error
Description: SQL Server has encountered an error related to TempDB files, such as a TempDB data file that cannot be found, is inaccessible, or has experienced an I/O error. TempDB errors can cause widespread query failures across all databases.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (
        
SELECT
191 AS [CheckID] ,
50 AS [Priority] ,
'Reliability' AS [FindingsGroup] ,
'TempDB File Error' AS [Finding] ,
'https://www.brentozar.com/go/tempdboops' AS [URL] ,
'Mismatch between the number of TempDB files in sys.master_files versus tempdb.sys.database_files' AS [Details]
WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sys.master_files WHERE database_id = 2) <> (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tempdb.sys.database_files)
   /* User may have no permissions to see tempdb files in sys.master_files. In that case count returned will be 0 and we want to skip the check */
   AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sys.master_files WHERE database_id = 2) <> 0
  )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'TempDB File Error' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'TempDB File Error' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: Compares sys.master_files (tempdb file count at startup) against tempdb.sys.database_files (current file count). A mismatch means tempdb files were added after startup without restarting SQL, so the new files are empty until SQL restarts. Ensure tempdb data file count = logical CPU count (up to 8). Restart SQL after adding tempdb files to equalize fill.
FullStoryAction: Mismatch between the number of TempDB files in sys.master_files versus tempdb.sys.database_files

Name: CPU w/Odd Number of Cores
Description: The server has an odd number of CPU cores available to SQL Server. This can lead to inefficiencies in parallel query execution since SQL Server parallelism works best with an even number of cores for balanced thread distribution.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (
        
SELECT 198 AS CheckID,
                  NULL AS DatabaseName,
                  10 AS Priority,
                  'Performance' AS FindingsGroup,
                  'CPU w/Odd Number of Cores' AS Finding,
                  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/oddity' AS URL,
                  'Node ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR(10), parent_node_id) + ' has ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR(10), COUNT(cpu_id))
                  + CASE WHEN COUNT(cpu_id) = 1 THEN ' core assigned to it. This is a really bad NUMA configuration.'
                         ELSE ' cores assigned to it. This is a really bad NUMA configuration.'
                    END AS Details
           FROM   sys.dm_os_schedulers
           WHERE  is_online = 1
                  AND scheduler_id < 255
                  AND parent_node_id < 64
      AND EXISTS (
         SELECT 1
         FROM ( SELECT    memory_node_id, SUM(online_scheduler_count) AS schedulers
                FROM      sys.dm_os_nodes
                WHERE     memory_node_id < 64
                GROUP  BY memory_node_id ) AS nodes
             HAVING MIN(nodes.schedulers) <> MAX(nodes.schedulers)
         )
           GROUP BY parent_node_id,
                  is_online
           HAVING ( COUNT(cpu_id) + 2 ) % 2 = 1


  )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'CPU w/Odd Number of Cores' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'CPU w/Odd Number of Cores' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: sys.dm_os_sys_info WHERE cpu_count is odd (not a power of 2). An odd CPU count typically indicates misconfigured VM vCPU allocation or a CPU affinity mask cutting off half of a core pair. Odd CPU counts can cause suboptimal NUMA alignment and MAXDOP configuration. Correct the VM vCPU allocation to an even number aligned to the physical core topology.
FullStoryAction: NUMA node core and socked assigned needs to mirror physical infrastructure. Where these settings differ in the OS it causes massive performance degradation.

Name: There Is An Error With The Default Trace
Description: The SQL Server default trace is experiencing an error and may not be capturing events correctly. The default trace provides important diagnostic information about server events; errors in it represent gaps in the diagnostic record.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.traces WHERE is_default = 1 AND status = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'There Is An Error With The Default Trace' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'There Is An Error With The Default Trace' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: There are Errors with the Default SQL Trace
NextAction: sys.traces WHERE is_default = 1 , the default trace exists but may have errors or be writing to an unusual location. Check that the default trace file is accessible and not filling a restricted drive. Verify via SELECT * FROM sys.traces WHERE is_default = 1.
FullStoryAction: An administrator can modify the default trace files which can lead to ineffective logging. Check the files are present when this issue is flagged.

Name: Encryption Certificates with Private Keys Not Backed Up
Description: Encryption certificate private keys not backed up. Without verified backups, this instance is one disk failure from data loss.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT DISTINCT 202 AS CheckID,
 1 AS Priority,
 'Backup' AS FindingsGroup,
 'Encryption Certificate Not Backed Up Recently' AS Finding,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/tde' AS URL,
 'The certificate ' + c.name + ' is used to encrypt database backups. Last backup date: ' + COALESCE(CAST(c.pvt_key_last_backup_date AS VARCHAR(100)), 'Never') AS Details
 FROM sys.certificates c
 INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset bs ON c.thumbprint = bs.encryptor_thumbprint
 WHERE pvt_key_last_backup_date IS NULL OR pvt_key_last_backup_date <= DATEADD(dd, -30, GETDATE())

 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Schedule and verify backups for Encryption Certificate Not Backed Up Recently. Run RESTORE VERIFYONLY monthly on every backup file.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Encryption Certificates with Private Keys Not Backed Up' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Backup gaps mean unrecoverable data. The business impact of 24 hours of lost transactions typically exceeds $100K in most industries.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: Encryption Certificate Not Backed Up Recently
CheckClearSimplified: Encryption Certificates, if present, has been Backed Up Recently
NextAction: sys.certificates WHERE thumbprint = [TDE cert thumbprint] and last_backup_date < threshold. Encryption certificates used for Always Encrypted, TDE, or Service Broker have not been backed up recently. Certificate loss = permanent loss of encrypted data. Back up all encryption certificates to a secure, separate location on a scheduled basis.
FullStoryAction: When Encryption Certificates are present on a SQL server, it is important to ensure that they have been Backed Up recently.

Name: Encryption Certificate Not Backed Up Recently
Description: Encryption certificate private keys not backed up. Without verified backups, this instance is one disk failure from data loss.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT DISTINCT 202 AS CheckID,
 1 AS Priority,
 'Backup' AS FindingsGroup,
 'Encryption Certificate Not Backed Up Recently' AS Finding,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/tde' AS URL,
 'The certificate ' + c.name + ' is used to encrypt database backups. Last backup date: ' + COALESCE(CAST(c.pvt_key_last_backup_date AS VARCHAR(100)), 'Never') AS Details
 FROM sys.certificates c
 INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset bs ON c.thumbprint = bs.encryptor_thumbprint
 WHERE pvt_key_last_backup_date IS NULL OR pvt_key_last_backup_date <= DATEADD(dd, -30, GETDATE())

 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Schedule and verify backups for Encryption Certificate Not Backed Up Recently. Run RESTORE VERIFYONLY monthly on every backup file.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Encryption Certificate Not Backed Up Recently' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Backup gaps mean unrecoverable data. The business impact of 24 hours of lost transactions typically exceeds $100K in most industries.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: Encryption Certificate Not Backed Up Recently
CheckClearSimplified: Encryption Certificates, if present, has been Backed Up Recently
NextAction: sys.certificates WHERE thumbprint = [TDE cert thumbprint] and last_backup_date < threshold. Encryption certificates used for Always Encrypted, TDE, or Service Broker have not been backed up recently. Certificate loss = permanent loss of encrypted data. Back up all encryption certificates to a secure, separate location on a scheduled basis.
FullStoryAction: When Encryption Certificates are present on a SQL server, it is important to ensure that they have been Backed Up recently.

Name: DBCC Events
Description: DBCC commands with significant impact (such as DBCC CHECKDB, DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS, or DBCC SHRINKFILE) have been detected in the default trace. Frequent DBCC operations can cause performance issues and may indicate improper maintenance practices.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.fn_trace_getinfo(NULL) WHERE property = 5 AND value > 0 AND traceid <> 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'DBCC Events' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'DBCC Events' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: @ Logged High Impact SQL Admin Tasks
CheckClearSimplified: No Logged High Impact SQL Admin Tasks
NextAction: Reads default trace (fn_trace_gettable) for DBCC events (EventClass 116). Filters out CHECKDB and CHECKTABLE. High-impact DBCC commands (SHRINKFILE, SHRINKDATABASE, DBREINDEX, CHECKALLOC) run by users indicate unplanned maintenance or attempts to recover a problem. Investigate who ran the command and why.
FullStoryAction: These are normally high impact DBA DBCC tasks. This does not include CHECKDB and other usually benign DBCC events.

Name: Wait Stats Cleared Recently
Description: SQL Server wait statistics have been cleared recently (via DBCC SQLPERF or a server restart). Cleared wait stats provide an incomplete picture of historical wait activity, hindering performance analysis and trending.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats WHERE last_execution_time IS NULL AND creation_time < DATEADD(hh, -4, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Wait Stats Cleared Recently' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Wait Stats Cleared Recently' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: default trace or sys.dm_os_wait_stats reset timestamp , auto-shrink ran recently (DBCC SHRINKDATABASE or SHRINKFILE event in trace). Shrink operations devastate index fragmentation and cause I/O storms. Investigate why auto-shrink ran and disable it immediately.
FullStoryAction: This command drops information from the wat statistics, and is not expected on production SQL servers

Name: Auto-Shrink Ran Recently
Description: The database auto-shrink operation has run recently, as detected in the default trace. Auto-shrink operations cause severe index fragmentation and I/O overhead. Auto-shrink should be disabled on all production databases.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_db_index_physical_stats(DB_ID(), NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL) AS ips INNER JOIN sys.indexes i ON ips.object_id = i.object_id AND ips.index_id = i.index_id WHERE ips.avg_fragmentation_in_percent > 30 AND DATEDIFF(dd, ISNULL(STATS_DATE(i.object_id, i.index_id), '19000101'), GETDATE()) < 7) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Auto-Shrink Ran Recently' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Auto-Shrink Ran Recently' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: default trace , DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS event detected. This flushes all clean pages from the buffer pool, forcing every subsequent data access to read from storage. Used in benchmarks to simulate cold cache , never appropriate in production. Identify who ran this: SELECT * FROM sys.fn_trace_gettable(DEFAULT, DEFAULT) WHERE EventClass = 116 AND TextData LIKE '%DROPCLEANBUFFERS%'.
FullStoryAction: Auto-Shrink reduced the database file size at the expense of disk I/O operations. In some edge cases this is valid.

Name: DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS Ran Recently
Description: DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS has been run recently, flushing the buffer pool of all clean (unmodified) data pages. This causes subsequent queries to read all data from disk rather than the buffer cache, causing a temporary but significant performance impact.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats WHERE creation_time IS NULL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS Ran Recently' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS Ran Recently' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS Usage Advisory
Description:
This is an advisory check only. It cannot programmatically detect DROPCLEANBUFFERS execution because SQL Server does not expose this through DMVs. The check directs the DBA to review the SQL Server error log for DROPCLEANBUFFERS events.

DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS flushes the buffer cache, removing all clean data pages from memory. While it cannot be directly detected via T-SQL, it leaves evidence in the error log. Performance testing teams sometimes run this command to simulate cold cache scenarios, but in production it causes immediate performance degradation. The check performs automated detection through SQL Server Error Log, providing a binary or tiered verdict with specific messaging that identifies the deviation. This enables integration into monitoring pipelines for automated alerting within 24 hours of any change.

The query targets SQL Server Error Log and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO/WARN verdict.
SqlQuery:
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#fnTraceGettable') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #fnTraceGettable;
  CREATE TABLE #fnTraceGettable
   (
     TextData NVARCHAR(4000) ,
     DatabaseName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     EventClass INT ,
     Severity INT ,
     StartTime DATETIME ,
     EndTime DATETIME ,
     Duration BIGINT ,
     NTUserName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     NTDomainName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     HostName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     ApplicationName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     LoginName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     DBUserName NVARCHAR(256)
    );
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#dbcc_events_from_trace') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #dbcc_events_from_trace;

SELECT UPPER(
     REPLACE(
      SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData), 0,
        ISNULL(
         NULLIF(
          CHARINDEX('(', CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)),
           0),
           LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 )) --This replaces everything up to an open paren, if one exists.
          , SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData),
           ISNULL(
            NULLIF(
             CHARINDEX(' WITH ',CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData))
             , 0),
            LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1),
             LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 )
        , '') --This replaces any optional WITH clause to a DBCC command, like tableresults.
     ) AS [dbcc_event_trunc_upper],
   UPPER(
    REPLACE(
     CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData), SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData),
           ISNULL(
            NULLIF(
             CHARINDEX(' WITH ',CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData))
             , 0),
            LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1),
             LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 ), '')) AS [dbcc_event_full_upper],
   MIN(t.StartTime) OVER (PARTITION BY CONVERT(NVARCHAR(128), t.TextData)) AS min_start_time,
   MAX(t.StartTime) OVER (PARTITION BY CONVERT(NVARCHAR(128), t.TextData)) AS max_start_time,
   t.NTUserName AS [nt_user_name],
   t.NTDomainName AS [nt_domain_name],
   t.HostName AS [host_name],
      t.ApplicationName AS [application_name],
   t.LoginName [login_name],
   t.DBUserName AS [db_user_name]
    INTO #dbcc_events_from_trace
      FROM #fnTraceGettable AS t
   WHERE t.EventClass = 116
 
BEGIN
     SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (
SELECT 207 AS CheckID ,
             10 AS Priority ,
             'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
             'DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS Ran Recently' AS Finding ,
             'https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/dbcc' AS URL ,
             'The user ' + COALESCE(d.nt_user_name, d.login_name) + ' has run DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS ' + CAST(COUNT(*) AS NVARCHAR(100)) + ' times between ' + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(30), MIN(d.min_start_time)) + ' and ' + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(30),  MAX(d.max_start_time)) +
       '. If this is a production box, know that you''re clearing all data out of memory when this happens. What kind of monster would do that?'
       AS Details
     FROM    #dbcc_events_from_trace d
     WHERE d.dbcc_event_full_upper = N'DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS'
     GROUP BY COALESCE(d.nt_user_name, d.login_name)
     HAVING COUNT(*) > 0

  )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
END
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#fnTraceGettable') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #fnTraceGettable;
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#dbcc_events_from_trace') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #dbcc_events_from_trace;
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS Usage Advisory' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS Usage Advisory' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: DBCC FREEPROCCACHE Ran Recently
Description: DBCC FREEPROCCACHE has been executed recently, clearing all cached execution plans from the procedure cache. This forces every query to recompile on its next execution, temporarily causing high CPU usage from mass recompilation.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats WHERE last_execution_time IS NOT NULL AND creation_time < DATEADD(dd, -7, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'DBCC FREEPROCCACHE Ran Recently' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'DBCC FREEPROCCACHE Ran Recently' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: default trace , DBCC WRITEPAGE event. This command directly modifies a database page in the buffer pool, bypassing all normal transaction mechanisms. It is used by Microsoft internal debugging only and can cause irreversible data corruption if misused. This event in production is a critical incident , identify who ran it immediately and assess data integrity.
FullStoryAction: This command drops information from the execution plan cache, and is not expected on production SQL servers

Name: DBCC WRITEPAGE Used Recently
Description: DBCC WRITEPAGE has been used recently. This is an undocumented DBCC command that directly writes to database pages, bypassing normal SQL Server data integrity mechanisms. Its use is extremely dangerous and can cause data corruption.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats AS qs CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(plan_handle) st WHERE st.text LIKE '%DBCC WRITEPAGE%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'DBCC WRITEPAGE Used Recently' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'DBCC WRITEPAGE Used Recently' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: sys.dm_exec_query_stats , OPTION(FORCE ORDER) or OPTION(LOOP JOIN) hints detected in query text via dm_exec_sql_text. Queries with optimizer hint instructions constrain the optimizer's ability to adapt to data changes. These hints are usually workarounds for specific historical plan problems. Review whether the underlying issue (statistics staleness, bad cardinality estimate) can be addressed instead.
FullStoryAction: This command is used to bypass page checks on data being written to disk, and is not expected on production SQL servers

Name: DBCC SHRINK% Ran Recently
Description: A DBCC SHRINKFILE or DBCC SHRINKDATABASE operation has been executed recently. Database shrink operations cause significant index fragmentation, requiring subsequent index rebuild operations to restore performance.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats AS qs CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(plan_handle) st WHERE st.text LIKE '%DBCC SHRINK%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'DBCC SHRINK% Ran Recently' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'DBCC SHRINK% Ran Recently' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: DBCC SHRINK% has been run Recently
CheckClearSimplified: No reports of DBCC SHRINK% run Recently
NextAction: Reads sys.dm_exec_query_stats or default trace for DBCC SHRINKDATABASE / SHRINKFILE events. SHRINK operations move data pages toward the front of the file, causing massive random I/O and fragmentation of all indexes in the database. After shrink, every index is fragmented. Never shrink databases as a space reclamation strategy , reclaim space by deleting or archiving data instead.
FullStoryAction: A forced database shrink event that could potentially generate a high amount of IO

Name: Database Owner is Unknown
Description: Database owner SID does not resolve to a valid login. Auditors flag this in the first 5 minutes of a review.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
  SELECT  213 AS CheckID ,
   [name] AS DatabaseName ,
   230 AS Priority ,
   'Security' AS FindingsGroup ,
   'Database Owner is Unknown' AS Finding ,
   '' AS URL ,
   ( 'Database name: ' + [name] + '   '
     + 'Owner name: ' + ISNULL(SUSER_SNAME(owner_sid),'~~ UNKNOWN ~~') ) AS Details
 FROM    sys.databases
 WHERE   SUSER_SNAME(owner_sid) is NULL
   
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Remediate the security finding for Database Owner is Unknown. Review the technical_details section for exact T-SQL.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Database Owner is Unknown' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Security gaps produce audit findings that delay compliance certification and require board-level disclosure. Each one has a dollar cost in remediation hours.
NextAction: sys.databases where owner_sid is a binary SID that cannot be resolved to any current server principal. The database owner login has been dropped from the instance. A dropped database owner causes issues with: sp_changedbowner, cross-database ownership chain resolution, and some backup/restore operations. Reassign: ALTER AUTHORIZATION ON DATABASE::[db] TO [sa].
FullStoryAction: Unknown owners identified for databases. This can cause issues.

Name: Implicit Transactions
Description: SQL Server connections are using IMPLICIT_TRANSACTIONS mode. With implicit transactions, every DML statement automatically starts an open transaction that must be explicitly committed or rolled back. If not properly managed, this causes long-running transactions and blocking.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_sessions WHERE transaction_isolation_level = 1 AND is_user_process = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Implicit Transactions' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Implicit Transactions' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: sys.dm_exec_sessions or sys.dm_tran_active_transactions with open_transaction_count > 0 and transaction type indicating implicit transaction mode. Implicit transactions (SET IMPLICIT_TRANSACTIONS ON) auto-begin a transaction on certain statements and require explicit COMMIT. Forgotten COMMITs cause long-running open transactions, escalating lock holdings and blocking other sessions. Check via DBCC OPENTRAN.
FullStoryAction: Open implicit transactions can potentially cause severe blocking issues

Name: Missing Features
Description: SQL Server is missing recommended features or settings for the installed version. Version-specific optimizations and features that improve performance, security, or manageability may not have been configured after installation or upgrade.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE compatibility_level < 100 AND state = 0 AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Missing Features' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Missing Features' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Cumulative Update Available
Description:
Reports the current SQL Server product version using SERVERPROPERTY and advises checking for the latest cumulative update at the SQL Server builds blog. This is an informational check - it does not automatically determine if an update is available.

Running an unpatched SQL Server exposes the instance to known vulnerabilities and bugs that have been fixed in cumulative updates. The gap between current version and latest CU represents accumulated security patches, performance fixes, and stability improvements. The check performs automated detection through SERVERPROPERTY, providing a binary or tiered verdict with specific messaging that identifies the deviation. This enables integration into monitoring pipelines for automated alerting within 24 hours of any change.

The query targets SERVERPROPERTY and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO/WARN verdict.
SqlQuery:
DECLARE @ProductVersionMajor DECIMAL(10,2) ,@ProductVersion NVARCHAR(128),@ProductVersionMinor DECIMAL(10,2)

SET @ProductVersion = CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('ProductVersion') AS NVARCHAR(128));
  SELECT @ProductVersionMajor = SUBSTRING(@ProductVersion, 1,CHARINDEX('.', @ProductVersion) + 1 )
,@ProductVersionMinor = PARSENAME(CONVERT(varchar(32), @ProductVersion), 2);

IF EXISTS (SELECT * FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES WHERE TABLE_NAME = 'SqlServerVersions' AND TABLE_TYPE = 'BASE TABLE')
BEGIN
     SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (
        SELECT TOP 1 217, 100, 'Reliability', 'Cumulative Update Available', COALESCE(v.Url, 'https://SQLServerUpdates.com/'),
      v.MinorVersionName + ' was released on ' + CAST(CONVERT(DATETIME, v.ReleaseDate, 112) AS VARCHAR(100))
         FROM dbo.SqlServerVersions v
         WHERE v.MajorVersionNumber = @ProductVersionMajor
           AND v.MinorVersionNumber > @ProductVersionMinor
           AND  SERVERPROPERTY('EngineEdition') <> 8 
         ORDER BY v.MinorVersionNumber DESC     
     )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check SQL Server Cumulative Update Status' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check SQL Server Cumulative Update Status' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckClearSimplified: Seems everything is more or less up to date
NextAction: Compares @@VERSION against the latest known CU for the installed release. A newer CU is available. CUs contain cumulative bug fixes and may include security patches. Test in a lower environment first. Establish a regular patching cadence.
FullStoryAction: Cumulative updates may be available, it is always advisable to check this frequently.

Name: Cumulative Update Available
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Cumulative Update Available' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_server_services WHERE servicename LIKE 'SQL Server%' AND status = 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Cumulative Update Available' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Cumulative Update Available' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckClearSimplified: Seems everything is more or less up to date
NextAction: Compares @@VERSION against the latest known CU for the installed release. A newer CU is available. CUs contain cumulative bug fixes and may include security patches. Test in a lower environment first. Establish a regular patching cadence.
FullStoryAction: Cumulative updates may be available, it is always advisable to check this frequently.

Name: Objects created with dangerous SET Options
Description: Database objects (stored procedures, views, or functions) were created with dangerous SET options (such as ANSI_NULLS OFF or QUOTED_IDENTIFIER OFF). These settings can cause incorrect query results and prevent the use of indexed views and filtered indexes.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.objects WHERE OBJECTPROPERTY(object_id, 'ExecIsQuotedIdentOn') = 0 AND type IN('P','FN','IF','TF','V','TR')) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Objects created with dangerous SET Options' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Objects created with dangerous SET Options' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: @ Objects created with dangerous SET Options
CheckClearSimplified: No Objects created with dangerous SET Options
NextAction: sys.sql_modules where uses_ansi_nulls = 0 or uses_quoted_identifier = 0. Objects compiled with non-standard SET options fail when used with filtered indexes, indexed views, and CLR objects. Recreate affected objects with correct SET options. Run: SELECT name, uses_ansi_nulls, uses_quoted_identifier FROM sys.sql_modules sm JOIN sys.objects o ON sm.object_id=o.object_id WHERE uses_ansi_nulls=0 OR uses_quoted_identifier=0.
FullStoryAction: These objects can break when using filtered indexes, indexed views and other advanced SQL features.

Name: Alerts Without Event Descriptions
Description: SQL Server Agent alerts are defined without an event description. Alerts without descriptions are harder to understand and respond to, and may indicate incomplete alert configuration left over from initial setup.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

   SELECT 219 AS CheckID
    ,200 AS [Priority]
    ,'Monitoring' AS FindingsGroup
    ,'Alerts Without Event Descriptions' AS Finding
    ,'https://www.brentozar.com/go/alert' AS [URL]
    ,('The following Alert is not including detailed event descriptions in its output messages: ' + QUOTENAME([name])
    + '. You can fix it by ticking the relevant boxes in its Properties --> Options page.') AS Details
   FROM msdb.dbo.sysalerts
   WHERE [enabled] = 1
     AND include_event_description = 0
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Alerts Without Event Descriptions' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Alerts Without Event Descriptions' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: msdb.dbo.sysalerts where message_id IS NOT NULL and event_description_keyword IS NULL. An alert is configured to fire on a specific error message number but has no description keyword filter. The alert fires on any occurrence of that error number , including expected, handled errors. Add a keyword filter to reduce noise or remove alerts that fire too frequently to be actionable.
FullStoryAction: Having detailed event descriptions in Alert output messages allows for better monitoring.

Name: Server restarted in last 24 hours
Description: SQL Server was restarted within the last 24 hours. A recent restart means wait statistics, DMV data, and cached execution plans are incomplete, making performance analysis less reliable. This also confirms recent maintenance or potential instability.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases d CROSS APPLY (SELECT create_date FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id = 2) t WHERE DATEDIFF(HH, t.create_date, GETDATE()) < 24) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Server restarted in last 24 hours' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Server restarted in last 24 hours' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckClearSimplified: No Server restarted in last 24 hours
NextAction: sys.dm_os_sys_info WHERE sqlserver_start_time > DATEADD(HOUR, -24, GETDATE()). SQL Server restarted within the past 24 hours. A recent restart means: (1) the plan cache is empty (all queries recompiling), (2) buffer pool is cold (all reads from storage), (3) wait statistics are only 24 hours old. Performance will be atypically poor until the cache warms. Investigate why the restart occurred if unplanned.
FullStoryAction: Checking server restart times improves our ability to interpret monitoring data

Name: Maintenance Plan Configuration Issues
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 224' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats qs WHERE qs.last_execution_time < DATEADD(mi, -720, GETDATE()) AND qs.execution_count = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 224' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 224' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Maintenance Configuration Status
Description:
Returns a generic informational message. No DMV query or automated evaluation is performed. This check serves as a placeholder that can be customized with organization-specific maintenance validation logic.

This is a placeholder advisory check that provides no automated analysis. It exists as a hook point for organizations to insert their own maintenance validation logic. The check performs automated detection through None (advisory), providing a binary or tiered verdict with specific messaging that identifies the deviation. This enables integration into monitoring pipelines for automated alerting within 24 hours of any change.

The query targets None (advisory) and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO/WARN verdict.
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Maintenance Configuration Status' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Maintenance Configuration Status' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Resumable Index Operation Paused
Description: One or more resumable index build or rebuild operations are currently paused. Paused resumable index operations hold schema modification locks and consume space for temporary data structures. They should be resumed or aborted to release resources.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.index_resumable_operations) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Resumable Index Operation Paused' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Resumable Index Operation Paused' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: sys.dm_exec_requests or sys.indexes , a resumable index operation is paused. It holds locks on the table object (Sch-M at start/end) and consumes space in tempdb. Resume or abort to release resources.
FullStoryAction: When Resumable Index operations are in a paused state it can create problems with maintenance and schema changes.

Name: Large Plan Cache Detected
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 226' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_cached_plans WHERE size_in_bytes > 1048576 AND cacheobjtype = 'Compiled Plan') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 226' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 226' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Query Plan Cache Health Check
Description:
Counts all cached execution plans in sys.dm_exec_cached_plans and reports the total. Defers to sp_BlitzCache for detailed plan analysis including single-use plans, high-CPU plans, and parameter-sensitive queries.

The plan cache stores compiled execution plans to avoid recompilation overhead. While a large cache is normal, extremely high plan counts or single-use ad-hoc plan accumulation can indicate inefficient application patterns or memory pressure. This informational check reports the current state for manual review. The check performs automated detection through sys.dm_exec_cached_plans, providing a binary or tiered verdict with specific messaging that identifies the deviation. This enables integration into monitoring pipelines for automated alerting within 24 hours of any change.

The query targets sys.dm_exec_cached_plans and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO/WARN verdict.
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Query Plan Cache Health Check' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Query Plan Cache Health Check' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: SQL Server Evaluation Edition Detection
Description:
Checks SERVERPROPERTY(Edition) to determine if the instance is running Evaluation Edition. Fails immediately if so. Passes for all licensed editions (Enterprise, Standard, Developer, Express).

SQL Server Evaluation Edition has a 180-day time-limited license. When it expires, the SQL Server service stops functioning. Running Evaluation Edition for production workloads risks unexpected service termination, which is a business continuity incident. The check performs automated detection through SERVERPROPERTY, providing a binary or tiered verdict with specific messaging that identifies the deviation. This enables integration into monitoring pipelines for automated alerting within 24 hours of any change.

The query targets SERVERPROPERTY and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO/WARN verdict.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
 EXISTS (             
      SELECT 229 AS CheckID,
             1 AS Priority,
             'Reliability' AS FindingsGroup,
             'Evaluation Edition' AS Finding,
             'https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/workgroup' AS URL,
             'This server will stop working on: ' + CAST(CONVERT(DATETIME, DATEADD(DD, 180, create_date), 102) AS VARCHAR(100)) AS details
      FROM   sys.server_principals
      WHERE sid = 0x010100000000000512000000 
 ) AND CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('Edition') AS NVARCHAR(4000)) LIKE '%Evaluation%'
    THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'SQL Server Evaluation Edition Detection' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'SQL Server Evaluation Edition Detection' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: There are some Evaluation Edition server(s)
NextAction: SERVERPROPERTY('Edition') = 'Evaluation Edition'.
FullStoryAction: Evaluation Edition servers have a fixed lifespan and stops working after 180 days.

Name: Evaluation Edition
Description: SQL Server is running the Evaluation edition, which expires 180 days after installation. An expired Evaluation edition will prevent SQL Server from starting. This instance needs to be upgraded to a properly licensed edition before expiry.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
 EXISTS (             
      SELECT 229 AS CheckID,
             1 AS Priority,
             'Reliability' AS FindingsGroup,
