             'Evaluation Edition' AS Finding,
             'https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/workgroup' AS URL,
             'This server will stop working on: ' + CAST(CONVERT(DATETIME, DATEADD(DD, 180, create_date), 102) AS VARCHAR(100)) AS details
      FROM   sys.server_principals
      WHERE sid = 0x010100000000000512000000 
 ) AND CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('Edition') AS NVARCHAR(4000)) LIKE '%Evaluation%'
    THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Evaluation Edition' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Evaluation Edition' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: There are some Evaluation Edition server(s)
NextAction: SERVERPROPERTY('Edition') = 'Evaluation Edition'.
FullStoryAction: Evaluation Edition servers have a fixed lifespan and stops working after 180 days.

Name: Invalid login defined with Windows Authentication
Description: One or more SQL Server logins mapped to Windows users or groups that no longer exist in Active Directory (invalid or deleted accounts). These orphaned logins pose a security risk and should be removed to prevent unauthorized access attempts.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_principals WHERE type = 'S' AND principal_id > 4 AND name NOT LIKE '##%' AND name NOT LIKE 'NT %') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Invalid login defined with Windows Authentication' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Invalid login defined with Windows Authentication' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: @ Invalid users found with no matching valid AD login
CheckClearSimplified: All users have a matching valid AD login.
NextAction: sys.server_principals where type = 'U' and the Windows SID cannot be resolved (orphaned Windows login). The Windows account was deleted from Active Directory but the SQL login persists. Orphaned logins are a security exposure , if the AD account name is reused for a different user, they inherit all SQL permissions. Drop orphaned logins: DROP LOGIN [DOMAIN\username].
FullStoryAction: This occurs when a SQL Server user no longer exists in Active Directory. This causes things to fail.

Name: Service Broker Enabled on a Database
Description: Detects databases where Service Broker is enabled, which deviates from the SQL Server default (disabled). Service Broker provides asynchronous message queuing and reliable messaging between databases. While it is a powerful feature for building distributed applications, enabling it creates additional background processing overhead and requires careful management of message queues, transmission queues, and endpoints. This check surfaces databases with non-default broker configuration for review.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.databases d
    WHERE d.database_id > 4
      AND DB_NAME(d.database_id) != 'rdsadmin'
      AND d.[state] = 0
      AND (d.is_broker_enabled <> 0 OR d.is_broker_enabled IS NULL)
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Disable Service Broker if not actively used for messaging or asynchronous processing.
If Service Broker is required, ensure transmission queues are monitored and maintained.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no user databases have Service Broker enabled. Returns FAIL otherwise.
NextAction: sys.databases is_broker_enabled = 1. Service Broker provides asynchronous, reliable message queuing within SQL Server. If not actively used, an enabled broker adds background activity. If used, monitor sys.transmission_queue for poison messages and ensure conversation lifetime management (END CONVERSATION) is implemented. Poison messages can stop a queue entirely.
FullStoryAction: Service Broker is Enabled for this database.

Name: Unused Indexes Detected
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 232' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT i.name, OBJECT_NAME(i.object_id) AS tbl, s.user_seeks, s.user_scans, s.user_lookups FROM sys.indexes i LEFT JOIN sys.dm_db_index_usage_stats s ON i.object_id = s.object_id AND i.index_id = s.index_id AND s.database_id = DB_ID() WHERE i.type > 1 AND i.is_primary_key = 0 AND i.is_unique_constraint = 0 AND i.is_unique = 0 AND (s.user_seeks + s.user_scans + s.user_lookups) = 0 AND s.user_updates > 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 232' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 232' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Detect Unused or Rarely-Used Indexes
Description:
Joins sys.indexes with sys.dm_db_index_usage_stats for the current database. Identifies user indexes that have never been used for user reads (no seeks, scans, or lookups recorded since last restart) or have been used fewer than 10 times total.

Unused indexes consume disk space, slow down INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE operations (every index must be maintained on data changes), and waste buffer pool memory caching index pages that are never read. Rarely-used indexes (fewer than 10 user operations) are maintenance overhead with minimal benefit. The check performs automated detection through sys.indexes + sys.dm_db_index_usage_stats, providing a binary or tiered verdict with specific messaging that identifies the deviation. This enables integration into monitoring pipelines for automated alerting within 24 hours of any change.

The query targets sys.indexes + sys.dm_db_index_usage_stats and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO/WARN verdict.
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Detect Unused or Rarely-Used Indexes' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Detect Unused or Rarely-Used Indexes' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Memory Leak in USERSTORE_TOKENPERM Cache
Description: SQL Server's USERSTORE_TOKENPERM cache (used for permission token storage) is consuming an unusually large amount of memory. This known pattern typically occurs in environments with many ad-hoc queries, frequent permission changes, or cross-database ownership chains.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (             
SELECT 233 AS CheckID,
              50 AS Priority,
              'Performance' AS FindingsGroup,
              'Memory Leak in USERSTORE_TOKENPERM Cache' AS Finding,
              'https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/userstore' AS URL,
              N'UserStore_TokenPerm clerk is using ' + CAST(CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN type = 'USERSTORE_TOKENPERM' AND name = 'TokenAndPermUserStore' THEN pages_kb * 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) / 1024.0 / 1024.0 AS INT) AS NVARCHAR(100)) 
             + N'GB RAM, total buffer pool is ' + CAST(CAST(SUM(pages_kb) / 1024.0 / 1024.0 AS INT) AS NVARCHAR(100)) + N'GB.'
           AS details
       FROM sys.dm_os_memory_clerks
       HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN type = 'USERSTORE_TOKENPERM' AND name = 'TokenAndPermUserStore' THEN pages_kb * 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) / SUM(pages_kb) >= 0.1     
         AND SUM(pages_kb) / 1024.0 / 1024.0 >= 1 /* At least 1GB RAM overall */
  ) AND EXISTS (SELECT * FROM sys.all_columns WHERE object_id = OBJECT_ID('sys.dm_os_memory_clerks') AND name = 'pages_kb')
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Memory Leak in USERSTORE_TOKENPERM Cache' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Memory Leak in USERSTORE_TOKENPERM Cache' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: sys.database_scoped_configurations XTP_PROCEDURE_EXECUTION_STATISTICS = ON. Collects module-level execution statistics for natively compiled T-SQL. Adds overhead proportional to natively compiled procedure call rate. Enable temporarily for In-Memory OLTP performance investigation; disable in production.
FullStoryAction: The USERSTORE_TOKENPERM cache can grow out and put enough pressure on SQL Server that it can no longer cache any plans.

Name: SQL Server Update May Fail
Description: A condition exists that may cause a future SQL Server update or patch to fail. This could be related to database files in an unusual state, file permission issues, or other conditions that interfere with the update installer process.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
  EXISTS (             
    SELECT 234 AS CheckID,
           100 AS Priority,
        db_name(f.database_id) AS DatabaseName,
           'Reliability' AS FindingsGroup,
           'SQL Server Update May Fail' AS Finding,
           'https://desertdba.com/failovers-cant-serve-two-masters/' AS URL,
           'This database has a file with a logical name of ''master'', which can break SQL Server updates. Rename it in SSMS by right-clicking on the database, go into Properties, and rename the file. Takes effect instantly.' AS details
    FROM master.sys.master_files f
    WHERE (f.name = N'master')
      AND f.database_id > 4
      AND db_name(f.database_id) <> 'master'
  ) 
     THEN 1
  ELSE 0
 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'SQL Server Update May Fail' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'SQL Server Update May Fail' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: SQL Server updates may fail on @ server(s)
NextAction: sys.database_scoped_configurations XTP_QUERY_EXECUTION_STATISTICS = ON. Statement-level statistics for natively compiled modules. Higher overhead than module-level. Use only during active In-Memory OLTP investigations.
FullStoryAction: Databases with a file which has a logical name of 'master',  can break SQL Server updates.

Name: Inconsistent Query Store metadata
Description: Query Store metadata in the master database is out of sync with the database-specific Query Store state. This inconsistency can cause unexpected Query Store behavior and may require the Query Store to be cleared or reset to resolve.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_query_store_options WHERE actual_state <> desired_state) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Inconsistent Query Store metadata'' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Inconsistent Query Store metadata'' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: sys.database_scoped_configurations ROW_MODE_MEMORY_GRANT_FEEDBACK (related). SQL 2019+ IQP. Row mode memory grant feedback adjusts grants between executions for row-mode operators. Leave enabled to allow the engine to self-correct memory grant over/under-allocation.
FullStoryAction: Query store state in master metadata and database specific metadata not in sync

Name: Snapshotting Too Many Databases
Description: SQL Server is creating database snapshots on a large number of databases simultaneously. Mass snapshotting can cause excessive I/O due to copy-on-write operations for all databases being snapshotted, impacting performance of production workloads.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT TOP 1 236 AS CheckID ,
 50 AS Priority ,
 'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Snapshotting Too Many Databases' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/toomanysnaps' AS URL ,
 ( CAST(SUM(1) AS VARCHAR(20)) + ' databases snapshotted at once in the last two weeks, indicating that IO may be freezing up. Microsoft does not recommend VSS snaps for 35 or more databases.') AS Details
FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs
WHERE bs.type = 'D'
AND bs.backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(DAY, -14, GETDATE()) /* In the last 2 weeks */
GROUP BY bs.backup_finish_date
HAVING SUM(1) >= 35
ORDER BY SUM(1) DESC
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Snapshotting Too Many Databases' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Snapshotting Too Many Databases' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckClearSimplified: No snapshot warnings on database count
NextAction: Row-Level Security predicate configuration. Confirm RLS predicates are correctly filtering rows for all access paths. RLS applied via inline TVF predicates , ensure the predicate TVF uses SCHEMABINDING to avoid implicit table scans and supports index seeks.
FullStoryAction: Snapshotting Too Many Databases may cause I/O  to freeze up. Microsoft does not recommend VSS snaps for 35 or more databases

Name: BATCH_MODE_ADAPTIVE_JOINS Non-Default Value
Description: Detects when the database-scoped configuration BATCH_MODE_ADAPTIVE_JOINS is not set to its default value of 1 (enabled). This feature allows the query optimizer to defer the choice of hash join or nested loop join algorithm until the first input has been scanned, improving plan quality for parameter-sensitive queries. Disabling it may degrade query performance for workloads that benefit from adaptive join selection.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.database_scoped_configurations dsc
    WHERE dsc.configuration_id = 9
      AND (CAST(dsc.value AS NVARCHAR(100)) <> '1' OR dsc.value IS NULL)
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Set BATCH_MODE_ADAPTIVE_JOINS to the default value of 1 unless directed otherwise by Microsoft support.
Run ALTER DATABASE SCOPED CONFIGURATION SET BATCH_MODE_ADAPTIVE_JOINS = ON in affected databases.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when BATCH_MODE_ADAPTIVE_JOINS is set to the default value of 1. Returns FAIL otherwise.

Name: DEFERRED_COMPILATION_TV Non-Default Value
Description: Detects when the database-scoped configuration DEFERRED_COMPILATION_TV is not set to its default value of 1 (enabled). This feature defers compilation of table-valued functions (TVFs) and table-valued parameters until the first execution time, allowing the optimizer to use actual cardinality estimates instead of default guesses. Disabling it reverts to the legacy behavior of using fixed cardinality estimates, which can cause poor query plan choices when TVFs return variable row counts.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.database_scoped_configurations dsc
    WHERE dsc.configuration_id = 19
      AND (CAST(dsc.value AS NVARCHAR(100)) <> '1' OR dsc.value IS NULL)
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Set DEFERRED_COMPILATION_TV to the default value of 1 using ALTER DATABASE SCOPED CONFIGURATION.
If disabled due to a plan regression, test with the latest SQL Server CU before keeping it off.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when DEFERRED_COMPILATION_TV is set to the default value of 1. Returns FAIL otherwise.

Name: LIGHTWEIGHT_QUERY_PROFILING Non-Default Value
Description: Detects when the database-scoped configuration LIGHTWEIGHT_QUERY_PROFILING is not set to its default value of 1 (enabled). This feature provides per-operator query execution statistics with minimal overhead and is the foundation for features like the Query Store wait statistics, last query plan stats, and the sys.dm_exec_query_stats infrastructure. Disabling it removes critical performance diagnostic capabilities from the database.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.database_scoped_configurations dsc
    WHERE dsc.configuration_id = 22
      AND (CAST(dsc.value AS NVARCHAR(100)) <> '1' OR dsc.value IS NULL)
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Set LIGHTWEIGHT_QUERY_PROFILING to the default value of 1 using ALTER DATABASE SCOPED CONFIGURATION.
If disabled, verify that no performance monitoring tool dependencies require this feature.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when LIGHTWEIGHT_QUERY_PROFILING is set to the default value of 1. Returns FAIL otherwise.

Name: Log Backups to NUL
Description: Transaction log backups are being taken but written to the NUL device, effectively discarding the backup. While this truncates the transaction log, the log backup is not recoverable, eliminating the ability to perform point-in-time recovery.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
       WHEN EXISTS (
       SELECT DISTINCT
 256 AS CheckID ,
 d.name AS DatabaseName,
 1 AS Priority ,
 'Backup' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Log Backups to NUL' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/nul' AS URL ,
 N'The transaction log file has been backed up ' + CAST((SELECT count(*)
  FROM msdb.dbo.backupset AS b INNER JOIN
  msdb.dbo.backupmediafamily AS bmf
   ON b.media_set_id = bmf.media_set_id
  WHERE b.database_name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS = d.name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
  AND bmf.physical_device_name = 'NUL'
  AND b.type = 'L'
  AND b.backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(dd,
   -7, GETDATE())) AS NVARCHAR(8)) + ' time(s) to ''NUL'' in the last week, which means the backup does not exist. This breaks point-in-time recovery.' AS Details
FROM master.sys.databases AS d
WHERE d.recovery_model IN ( 1, 2 )
 AND d.database_id NOT IN ( 2, 3 )
 AND d.source_database_id IS NULL
 AND d.state NOT IN(1, 6, 10) /* Not currently offline or restoring, like log shipping databases */
 AND d.is_in_standby = 0 /* Not a log shipping target database */
 AND d.source_database_id IS NULL /* Excludes database snapshots */
 --AND d.name NOT IN ( SELECT DISTINCT
 --  DatabaseName
 --  FROM #SkipChecks
 --  WHERE CheckID IS NULL OR CheckID = 2)
 AND EXISTS ( SELECT *
  FROM msdb.dbo.backupset AS b INNER JOIN
  msdb.dbo.backupmediafamily AS bmf
   ON b.media_set_id = bmf.media_set_id
  WHERE d.name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS = b.database_name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
  AND bmf.physical_device_name = 'NUL'
  AND b.type = 'L'
  AND b.backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(dd,
     -7, GETDATE()) )
       ) THEN 1
       ELSE 0
      END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Log Backups to NUL' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Log Backups to NUL' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: Log Backups to NUL, which means the backup does not exist
CheckClearSimplified: Log Backing up to normal locations
NextAction: msdb.dbo.backupset WHERE device_type = 6 (NUL device). Backups are being sent to NUL (the Windows null device) , they are being discarded. This is used in benchmarks to test throughput without writing data. In production, this means the 'backup' job runs and reports success but produces no backup file. Immediate action: fix the backup device to write to a real destination.
FullStoryAction: All Log Backups should target locations that are easily accessible by both the DBA and Backup Teams

Name: Recovery Interval Not Optimal
Description: The Recovery Interval server configuration option is set to a non-default value. An overly high value means longer recovery time after a crash; too low causes excessive I/O from frequent checkpoints impacting normal workload performance.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN  EXISTS(SELECT
               1/0
           FROM sys.all_columns AS ac
           WHERE ac.name = 'target_recovery_time_in_seconds'
           AND   ac.object_id = OBJECT_ID('sys.databases'))
     AND EXISTS ( 
     SELECT
  DatabaseName = 
                       d.name,
                   CheckId = 
                       257,
                   Priority = 
                       50,
                   FindingsGroup = 
                       N'Performance',
                   Finding =
                       N'Recovery Interval Not Optimal',
                   URL = 
                       N'https://sqlperformance.com/2020/05/system-configuration/0-to-60-switching-to-indirect-checkpoints',
                   Details = 
                       N'The database ' +
                       QUOTENAME(d.name) +
                       N' has a target recovery interval of ' +
                       RTRIM(d.target_recovery_time_in_seconds) + 
                       CASE
                           WHEN d.target_recovery_time_in_seconds = 0
                           THEN N', which is a legacy default, and should be changed to 60.'
                           WHEN d.target_recovery_time_in_seconds <> 0 
                           THEN N', which is probably a mistake, and should be changed to 60.'
                       END
               FROM sys.databases AS d
               WHERE d.database_id > 4
               AND   d.is_read_only = 0
               AND   d.is_in_standby = 0
               AND   d.target_recovery_time_in_seconds <> 60
    
  )
  THEN 1
  ELSE 0
 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Recovery Interval Not Optimal' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Recovery Interval Not Optimal' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckClearSimplified: Recovery intervals for databases seems optimal.
NextAction: sp_configure 'recovery interval (min)' is set to a value that results in infrequent checkpoints. Infrequent checkpoints reduce I/O overhead but increase crash recovery time. With modern SSDs, the checkpoint I/O is rarely a bottleneck. Set target_recovery_time at the database level for granular control: ALTER DATABASE [db] SET TARGET_RECOVERY_TIME = 60 SECONDS.
FullStoryAction: Recovery interval not optimal for some databases. This can negatively impact RPO.

Name: Dangerous Service Account - SQL Server Running as LocalSystem
Description: Detects when the SQL Server database engine service is running under the LocalSystem or NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM account. These accounts have unrestricted access to the local system, including all files, registry keys, and the ability to interact with the desktop. Any attacker who gains the ability to execute xp_cmdshell (or other OS-level commands) through SQL Server would have full administrative control over the host operating system. SQL Server should always run under a low-privilege domain account or virtual account.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.dm_server_services
    WHERE ([service_account] = 'LocalSystem'
       OR LOWER([service_account]) = 'nt authority\system')
      AND [servicename] LIKE 'SQL Server%'
      AND [servicename] NOT LIKE 'SQL Server Agent%'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Change SQL Server service account to a low-privilege domain or virtual account using SQL Server Configuration Manager.
Ensure xp_cmdshell is disabled unless explicitly required and properly secured.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when SQL Server is not running as LocalSystem. Returns FAIL when running under LocalSystem or NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: SQL Server is running under an NT Service account
CheckClearSimplified: SQL Server is running under an domain Service account
NextAction: sys.columns where encryption_type IS NOT NULL. Column-level encryption (CLE via EncryptByKey/EncryptByCert, or Always Encrypted via column master key). Informational , confirms encryption is in place for sensitive data. Verify key rotation is scheduled and backup of encryption keys/certificates is current (separate from database backup).
FullStoryAction: SQL Server services can run as NT Service accounts. It is ideal to run as Active Directory accounts.

Name: Dangerous Service Account - SQL Agent Running as LocalSystem
Description: Detects when the SQL Server Agent service is running under the LocalSystem or NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM account. SQL Agent executes scheduled jobs, including PowerShell and command shell steps, and running as LocalSystem means any job step can execute with full administrative privileges on the host. An attacker or malicious insider who can create or modify Agent jobs effectively has unrestricted OS-level access. SQL Agent should run under a dedicated low-privilege domain account with only the permissions required for its specific job tasks.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.dm_server_services
    WHERE ([service_account] = 'LocalSystem'
       OR LOWER([service_account]) = 'nt authority\system')
      AND [servicename] LIKE 'SQL Server Agent%'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Change SQL Agent service account to a low-privilege domain account using SQL Server Configuration Manager.
Grant only the specific permissions needed (e.g., access to network shares, specific directories) to the service account.
Use Proxy accounts for job steps requiring different security contexts.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when SQL Agent is not running as LocalSystem. Returns FAIL when running under LocalSystem or NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: SQL Server Agent is running under an NT Service account
CheckClearSimplified: SQL Server Agent is running under an domain Service account
NextAction: sys.dm_server_services WHERE servicename LIKE '%Agent%' and service_account LIKE 'NT Service%'. SQL Agent is running as the built-in NT Service account. This account has limited network access , it cannot write to network shares, access Active Directory, or perform remote operations required by some jobs (log shipping, SSIS, linked server jobs). Configure a dedicated domain service account with minimum required permissions.
FullStoryAction: SQL Server Agent services can run under NT Service accounts. It is ideal to run as Active Directory accounts.

Name: SQL Server Error Log Contains Critical Entries
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 260' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 260' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 260' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: SQL Server Error Log Health Advisory
Description:
Advisory check only. Directs the DBA to review the SQL Server error log via sp_readerrorlog for critical events. No automated analysis is performed because error log parsing requires pattern matching that is version-specific.

The SQL Server error log contains critical events: hardware errors, I/O failures, access violations, and stack dumps. Without periodic review, these events accumulate undetected until a failure occurs. This advisory directs the DBA to manually inspect the error log. The check performs automated detection through SQL Server Error Log, providing a binary or tiered verdict with specific messaging that identifies the deviation. This enables integration into monitoring pipelines for automated alerting within 24 hours of any change.

The query targets SQL Server Error Log and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO/WARN verdict.
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'SQL Server Error Log Health Advisory' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'SQL Server Error Log Health Advisory' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: SQL Server Agent Job Failures in Last 24 Hours
Description:
Joins msdb.dbo.sysjobsteps with msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory to count job step failures (run_status = 0) in the last 24 hours. Fails if any job step failure is detected.

SQL Server Agent jobs automate critical maintenance: backups, index rebuilds, statistics updates, integrity checks, and data warehouse ETL. A job failure that goes undetected for 24 hours means any of these tasks may have silently stopped. Backup job failure is the most serious - it creates a growing data-loss window. The check performs automated detection through msdb.dbo.sysjobsteps + sysjobhistory, providing a binary or tiered verdict with specific messaging that identifies the deviation. This enables integration into monitoring pipelines for automated alerting within 24 hours of any change.

The query targets msdb.dbo.sysjobsteps + sysjobhistory and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO/WARN verdict.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs j INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory h ON j.job_id = h.job_id WHERE j.enabled = 1 AND h.run_status = 0 AND h.run_date >= CONVERT(VARCHAR(8), DATEADD(dd, -1, GETDATE()), 112)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'SQL Server Agent Job Failures in Last 24 Hours' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'SQL Server Agent Job Failures in Last 24 Hours' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Failed SQL Agent Jobs Detected
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 261' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs j INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory h ON j.job_id = h.job_id WHERE j.enabled = 1 AND h.run_status = 0 AND h.run_date >= CONVERT(VARCHAR(8), DATEADD(dd, -1, GETDATE()), 112)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 261' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 261' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Query Store Wait Stats Disabled
Description: Query Store wait statistics capture is disabled on one or more databases. Wait stats in Query Store provide valuable context about why queries are slow (blocked, waiting on memory, CPU, I/O, etc.) without requiring separate data collection.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_query_store_options WHERE wait_stats_capture_mode = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Query Store Wait Stats Disabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Query Store Wait Stats Disabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
CheckTriggeredSimplified: @ query store(s) wait statistics are Disabled
CheckClearSimplified: Query store wait statistics seem enabled
NextAction: sys.databases where is_query_store_on = 1 and query_store_options.desired_state_desc = 'READ_WRITE' but actual_state_desc = 'READ_ONLY'. Query Store has been forced to read-only mode because it reached the max_storage_size_mb limit. New query data is no longer being captured. Expand the limit: ALTER DATABASE [db] SET QUERY_STORE (MAX_STORAGE_SIZE_MB = 4096). Flush old data: ALTER DATABASE [db] SET QUERY_STORE CLEAR.
FullStoryAction: The query store feature for tracking wait stats is very useful for tracking wait stats at a query level. This assists with performance tuning.

Name: Query Store Effectively Disabled
Description: Query Store is configured in a way that renders it effectively non-functional, such as being in error state or configured with a very small storage quota that has already been exceeded, causing it to switch to read-only mode.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_query_store_options WHERE actual_state = 0 AND desired_state = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Query Store Effectively Disabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Query Store Effectively Disabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: sys.database_query_store_options , Query Store configuration makes it effectively useless (e.g. QUERY_CAPTURE_MODE = NONE, or CLEANUP_POLICY with STALE_QUERY_THRESHOLD_DAYS = 1). Query Store is 'on' but not capturing anything meaningful. Set: QUERY_CAPTURE_MODE = AUTO, STALE_QUERY_THRESHOLD_DAYS = 30, MAX_STORAGE_SIZE_MB = 2048.
FullStoryAction: The database query store can be in a state where it is not writing new data, which makes it ineffective. Ideally all query stores should be actively recording data if there are active workloads.

Name: Undesired Query Store State
Description: Query Store is in an undesired state (for example, read-only due to exceeding storage quota, or in an error state). When in read-only mode, Query Store stops capturing new data, defeating its purpose as a performance history tool.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_query_store_options WHERE actual_state NOT IN(0,1)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Undesired Query Store State' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Undesired Query Store State' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: Query Store is in an undesired state , actual_state_desc does not match desired_state_desc. SQL Server may have automatically switched Query Store to read-only due to storage pressure. Investigate why: run SELECT * FROM sys.database_query_store_options to see current/desired state and max storage usage.
FullStoryAction: Sometimes the current running state does not match the desired state of a query store. This is not ideal

Name: Query Store Unusually Configured
Description: Query Store has unusual configuration settings that differ significantly from recommended values, such as a very short data retention period, overly aggressive cleanup settings, or non-standard collection intervals that may cause data loss or excessive overhead.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_query_store_options WHERE max_storage_size_mb < 1024 OR stale_query_threshold_days < 7) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Query Store Unusually Configured' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Query Store Unusually Configured' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: sys.database_query_store_options has unusual settings , e.g. INTERVAL_LENGTH_MINUTES too short (excess storage churn), MAX_PLANS_PER_QUERY too low (dropping plans prematurely), or WAIT_STATS_CAPTURE_MODE = OFF. Review and standardise Query Store configuration across all databases.
FullStoryAction: Query store has different capture modes. Some can hurt performance for certain workloads, especially ad-hoc queries

Name: AG Replica Falling Behind
Description: An Always On Availability Group secondary replica is falling behind the primary in log redo. A lagging secondary means the HA/DR protection window is widening, and in the event of a primary failure, data loss may occur.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (             
SELECT 268 AS CheckID,
              5 AS Priority,
           DB_NAME(ps.database_id),
              'Availability' AS FindingsGroup,
              'AG Replica Falling Behind' AS Finding,
              'https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/ag' AS URL,
              ag.name + N' AG replica server ' + 
          ar.replica_server_name + N' is ' + 
          CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(SECOND, ISNULL (drs.last_commit_time, drs.Last_hardened_time), ps.last_commit_time) < 200 THEN (CAST(DATEDIFF(SECOND, drs.last_commit_time, ps.last_commit_time) AS NVARCHAR(10)) + N' seconds ')
          ELSE (CAST(DATEDIFF(MINUTE, ISNULL (drs.last_commit_time, drs.Last_hardened_time), ps.last_commit_time) AS NVARCHAR(10)) + N' minutes ') END
          + N' behind the primary.'
          AS details
       FROM sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states AS drs
       JOIN sys.availability_replicas AS ar ON drs.replica_id = ar.replica_id
       JOIN sys.availability_groups AS ag ON ar.group_id = ag.group_id
       JOIN sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states AS ps 
        ON drs.group_id = ps.group_id 
        AND drs.database_id = ps.database_id
        AND ps.is_local = 1 /* Primary */
       WHERE drs.is_local = 0 /* Secondary */
         AND DATEDIFF(SECOND,ISNULL (drs.last_commit_time, drs.Last_hardened_time), ps.last_commit_time) > 60
  ) 
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'AG Replica Falling Behind' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'AG Replica Falling Behind' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
FullStoryAction: AG Replica server is falling behing Primary

Name: Memory Dangerous Low Recently
Description: SQL Server's available memory has recently dropped to dangerously low levels. Critically low memory causes queries to fail with out-of-memory errors, forces data pages out of the buffer cache, and can cause overall server instability.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_os_ring_buffers WHERE ring_buffer_type = 'RING_BUFFER_RESOURCE_MONITOR' AND CONVERT(XML, record).value('(./Record/ResourceMonitor/Indicators)[1]', 'varchar(max)') LIKE '%RESOURCE_MEMPHYSICAL_LOW%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Memory Dangerous Low Recently' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Memory Dangerous Low Recently' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
FullStoryAction: Memory issues reported in the last 4 hours

Name: TempDB Governor Config Problem
Description: TempDB has a configuration problem related to metadata optimization or governor settings. Misconfigurations can prevent TempDB from scaling properly under high concurrency workloads, leading to allocation contention and performance degradation.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_db_task_space_usage WHERE session_id > 50 GROUP BY session_id HAVING SUM(user_objects_alloc_page_count + internal_objects_alloc_page_count) > 10240) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'TempDB Governor Config Problem' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'TempDB Governor Config Problem' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
FullStoryAction: Resource Governor is configured to cap TempDB usage by percent, but the TempDB file configuration will not allow that to take effect.

Name: Optimized Locking Not Fully Set Up
Description: Optimized Locking (a SQL Server 2022+ feature) is available but not fully configured. For Optimized Locking to be fully effective, Read Committed Snapshot Isolation (RCSI) should also be enabled on databases. Without RCSI, the lock reduction benefits are limited.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE is_read_committed_snapshot_on = 0 AND database_id > 4 AND state = 0 AND source_database_id IS NULL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Optimized Locking Not Fully Set Up' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Optimized Locking Not Fully Set Up' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
FullStoryAction: RCSI should be enabled on this database to get the full benefits of optimized locking

Name: System Uptime

Regular validation ensures that drift from approved baselines is detected before it causes incidents.

Without regular validation, configuration drift accumulates silently across SQL Server instances. CheckTriggeredSimplified: This is a node in a cluster
CheckClearSimplified: This server is not a cluster node
NextAction: sys.servers where is_linked = 1. Linked servers expose the SQL instance to remote data sources via OLE DB. Each linked server connection uses a security context (mapped login or pass-through). Linked server queries bypass query parameterisation, cannot use local statistics, and can cause distributed deadlocks. Audit each linked server: SELECT * FROM sys.servers; verify the data_source, provider, and catalog. Disable unused linked servers.
FullStoryAction: Depending on how critical the workload is that runs on this machine, it might make sense to make it clustered.

Name: Duplicate Indexes to Drop
Description: Checks duplicate indexes to drop. This metric correlates directly with instance health and user-facing performance.
RecommendedAction: Merge or drop duplicate indexes that have identical key columns. Review using sys.dm_db_index_usage_stats to make sure the kept index has recent seeks.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Duplicate Indexes to Drop' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Index problems compound: bad indexes slow writes, waste storage, and confuse the query optimizer. This is a direct cost in disk, CPU, and developer troubleshooting time.

Name: Database Corruption Detected
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Database Corruption Detected' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
  WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.suspect_pages WHERE event_type IN (1, 2, 3))
  THEN 1
  ELSE 0
  END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Database Corruption Detected' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Database Corruption Detected' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
NextAction: Reads msdb.dbo.suspect_pages (page_error_type > 0) or sys.databases where state_desc = 'SUSPECT'. A database in SUSPECT state is inaccessible. Causes: I/O subsystem failure, hardware fault, abrupt shutdown. Recovery path: restore from backup (preferred), or DBCC CHECKDB WITH REPAIR_ALLOW_DATA_LOSS (last resort , data loss accepted).
FullStoryAction: This is only flagged if the server detects corruption in a database

Name: Blocking Detected

SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_os_waiting_tasks wt WHERE wt.blocking_session_id > 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END

Name: High Index Fragmentation
Description: Suboptimal 'High Index Fragmentation' degrades query throughput and increases resource contention. Users experience increased latency and potential timeouts during peak workloads.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
  WHEN EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 
  FROM sys.dm_db_index_physical_stats(DB_ID(), NULL, NULL, NULL, 'LIMITED') ips
  WHERE ips.avg_fragmentation_in_percent > 30
  AND ips.page_count > 1000
  AND ips.index_id > 0
  ) THEN 1
  ELSE 0
  END
RecommendedAction: Tune the query or configuration related to High Index Fragmentation. Check execution plan and wait statistics.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'High Index Fragmentation' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Bad indexing burns CPU and IO you already paid for. Users experience this as slow page loads and abandoned sessions.

Name: Missing Index Recommendations
Description: Checks if there are missing index recommendations with high impact
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
  WHEN EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 
  FROM sys.dm_db_missing_index_details mid
  INNER JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_groups mig ON mid.index_handle = mig.index_handle
  INNER JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_group_stats migs ON mig.index_group_handle = migs.group_handle
  WHERE migs.avg_user_impact > 50
  ) THEN 1
  ELSE 0
  END
RecommendedAction: Tune the query or configuration related to Missing Index Recommendations. Check execution plan and wait statistics.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Missing Index Recommendations' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Bad indexing burns CPU and IO you already paid for. Users experience this as slow page loads and abandoned sessions.
NextAction: sys.databases where state_desc = 'EMERGENCY'. Emergency mode is set manually (ALTER DATABASE SET EMERGENCY) to access a suspect database for forensic reads. A database left in emergency mode is read-only and not accessible to the application. Investigate the original failure, restore from backup, or run DBCC CHECKDB WITH REPAIR_ALLOW_DATA_LOSS.
FullStoryAction: One of the top resource-intensive queries may be dramatically improved by adding an index

Name: Weak Password Policies
Description: Checks if password policy enforcement is disabled for SQL logins
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
  WHEN EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 
  FROM sys.sql_logins 
  WHERE is_policy_checked = 0 
  AND name NOT IN ('sa', '##MS_PolicyEventProcessingLogin##')
  ) THEN 1
  ELSE 0
  END
RecommendedAction: Remediate the security finding for Weak Password Policies. Review the technical_details section for exact T-SQL.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Weak Password Policies' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Weak authentication is the root cause in 61% of data breaches (Verizon DBIR 2024). Auditors test for this first.

Name: Review Heap Table
Description: Review Heap Table (sp_triage CheckID 1, Priority 100)
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases d LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset b ON d.name = b.database_name AND b.type = 'D' WHERE d.database_id > 4 AND d.state = 0 AND (b.backup_finish_date IS NULL OR b.backup_finish_date < DATEADD(DAY, -7, GETDATE()))) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Review Heap Table' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Review Heap Table' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Review Log Space
Description: Review Log Space (sp_triage CheckID 2, Priority 100)
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases d WHERE d.database_id > 4 AND d.recovery_model = 1 AND d.state = 0 AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.backupset b WHERE b.database_name = d.name AND b.type = 'L' AND b.backup_finish_date > DATEADD(HOUR, -2, GETDATE()))) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Schedule and verify backups for Review Log Space. Run RESTORE VERIFYONLY monthly on every backup file.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Review Log Space' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Backup gaps mean unrecoverable data. The business impact of 24 hours of lost transactions typically exceeds $100K in most industries.

Name: Review Query Stats
Description:
This check validates a key SQL Server configuration or operational state by querying sys.dm_exec_query_stats. The setting or condition monitored here directly affects instance behavior, performance, or security posture. Regular validation ensures that drift from approved baselines is detected before it causes incidents.

Without regular validation, configuration drift accumulates silently across SQL Server instances. Changes made for one-time troubleshooting or testing are often never reverted, creating a growing gap between the documented baseline and the actual running state. The check provides an automated detection mechanism that can be integrated into monitoring pipelines, CI/CD deployment validation, and periodic compliance audits. When non-compliant values are detected, the check produces actionable output identifying the specific deviation rather than a generic warning.

The query targets sys.dm_exec_query_stats and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO verdict, and the message column identifies which specific items require attention.
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Review Query Stats' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Review Query Stats' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Excessive VLF Count
Description: Checks if any database has excessive Virtual Log Files (>50)
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT d.name, COUNT(*) AS VLFs FROM sys.databases d CROSS APPLY sys.dm_db_log_info(DB_ID(d.name)) li WHERE d.state = 0 AND d.database_id > 4 AND d.name <> 'tempdb' GROUP BY d.name HAVING COUNT(*) > 999) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
